title: Massive Rose-Watered Egyptian Lamb Tagine
description: A traditional Egyptian dish with a rich, aromatic flavor profile and tender lamb.
instructions: Heat oil in the bottom of a large clay tagine over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 8 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and sliced ginger, and cook for another 2 minutes.
Add the lamb pieces and cook until browned on all sides, about 10 minutes.
Add the cumin, cinnamon, paprika, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add 1 cup of water to the tagine and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 1 hour and 30 minutes, or until the lamb is tender.
Stir in the rose water and continue to simmer for another 10 minutes.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh parsley and accompanied by crusty bread.
Let the tagine rest for 10 minutes before serving to allow the flavors to meld together.
